query: Yes, please go ahead and make the swap to the cheapest Desk Lamp and put the $17.99 refund on a gift card. That’s the only change I want to make to this order.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user asks to swap the desk lamp in an order for the cheapest desk lamp available and wants to know the refund difference on a gift card.
assistant: The assistant asks to authenticate the order.
user: The user provides name, zip code, and order ID W9300146 instead of an email.
assistant: The assistant looks up the user and retrieves pending order #W9300146.
assistant: The assistant checks desk lamp product variants and reports that the cheapest available desk lamp costs $135.24, which is $17.99 less than the current lamp.
assistant: The assistant explains that the refund difference can be credited to a gift card.
user: The user explicitly asks to swap to the cheapest desk lamp and put the $17.99 difference on a gift card, confirming this is the only requested change.

candidate_answers.text: Got it — you’ve chosen the cheapest desk lamp option and confirmed the refund should go to a gift card, so I can update the pending order now.
The desired replacement variant, refund destination, and scope of the order change are all grounded in visible context, so the next step is to execute the pending-item modification.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a user-confirmation turn where the replacement variant and refund destination are already surfaced, so item modification is grounded and immediately executable.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible retail state. Scores prioritize changing the pending order item because the user has already selected the replacement and approved the refund handling.
notes: the query is taken verbatim from the visible user confirmation turn
history_summary excludes the later modification tool output and closing confirmation
best_action is modify_pending_order_items rather than calculate because the price difference has already been computed and accepted by the user
